question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockFileTransformer.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: transform()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a crafted BlockFile where hasRecordFile() returns true, causing transform() to return the embedded RecordFile directly without re-validating or re-transforming its fields, resulting in stale or attacker-influenced record data being exported to mirror nodes?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockFileTransformer.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: transform()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a scenario where blockFile.getBlockHeader() returns a header with all-zero hapiProtoVersion fields (major=0, minor=0, patch=0), causing the constructed RecordFile to carry incorrect HAPI version metadata that downstream mirror node consumers use to interpret transaction records, leading to incorrect record parsing?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockFileTransformer.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: transform()] Can an unprivileged external user cause transform() to set fileHash to StringUtils.EMPTY unconditionally, bypassing any hash integrity check on the exported RecordFile, so that mirror node consumers receive a RecordFile with an empty fileHash and cannot detect tampering with the file content?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockFileTransformer.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getRecordItems()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a BlockFile with an empty blockTransactions list, causing getRecordItems() to return Collections.emptyList() and the resulting RecordFile to have zero items, so that all transactions in the block are silently omitted from the mirror node export?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockFileTransformer.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getRecordItems()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the reverse-order processing loop (index from blockTransactions.size()-1 to 0) by injecting a BlockTransaction at a specific index whose state changes shadow those of adjacent transactions, causing incorrect intermediate token supply values to be computed and exported for preceding transactions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockFileTransformer.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getRecordItems()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the second forward pass (builders.size()-1 to 0) that sets the previous link to assign an incorrect RecordItem as the previous item for a given transaction, breaking the linked-list chain and causing mirror nodes to export records with wrong parent-child relationships?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockFileTransformer.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getRecordItems()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a BlockFile where blockTransactions contains duplicate entries for the same transaction, causing getRecordItems() to produce duplicate RecordItems in the exported list, leading to double-counting of transactions on mirror nodes?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockFileTransformer.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getRecordItems()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a BlockFile where the transactionIndex assigned to each RecordItem.builder() does not match the actual position in the final recordItems list (due to the double-reversal), causing mirror nodes to export records with incorrect transactionIndex values that break ordering assumptions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockFileTransformer.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: transform()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a BlockFile with a null or missing softwareVersion in the blockHeader, causing transform() to throw a NullPointerException when calling softwareVersion.getMajor(), which aborts the transformation and results in the block's records being entirely missing from the mirror node export?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockFileTransformer.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: transform()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a BlockFile where blockFile.getCount() is mismatched with the actual number of items in blockFile.getItems(), causing the exported RecordFile to carry an incorrect count field that misleads mirror node consumers about the number of transactions in the block?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockFileTransformer.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: transform()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a BlockFile where blockFile.getPreviousHash() is set to an arbitrary value, and since transform() copies it directly into the RecordFile without re-verification, cause mirror nodes to accept a RecordFile with a forged previousHash that breaks the hash chain integrity?
